title: What‚Äôs my Late Response Rate, and how do I keep it low?
bodyHtml:
The Late Response Rate reflects how quickly you act on customer service requests related to Trade-in orders. It‚Äôs an important indicator of customer satisfaction.
¬Ý

Jump to

How is my Late Response Rate calculated?
What happens if my Late Response Rate is too high?
‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚ÄãHow can I keep my Late Response Rate low?
‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚ÄãHow should I monitor my performance indicators?
¬Ý

How is my Late Response Rate calculated?

Your Late Response Rate directly reflects how quickly you respond to requests from your customers. Sellers have 24 hours to respond to customer requests via the Quality Charter. This timeline excludes weekends and bank holidays (in the country where you have your warehouse).

This is the Late Response Rate formula: the number of Trade-in orders with a late seller response, divided by all Trade-in orders with a customer message.

A valid response to a customer message includes:

  • An action like confirming an order, making a counteroffer, indicating that an item can't be processed, contacting a customer if their action is needed, or indicating a shipping issue
  • An answer posted in the chat

Your Late Response Rate is calculated every day, and it‚Äôs based on orders received in the last 30 days.
¬Ý

What happens if my Late Response Rate is too high?

If your Late Response Rate is too high, you‚Äôll enter a ‚Äúmonitoring period" where you have the opportunity to improve your rate. If your rate doesn‚Äôt improve enough, your daily Trade-in limit will be decreased. If the issue persists, you won‚Äôt be able to use the Trade-in service.

‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚ÄãLearn more about Late Response Rate limits and how the monitoring period works
¬Ý

How can I keep my Late Response Rate low?

We want you to keep killing it ‚Äî so here‚Äôs our advice on keeping your Late Response Rate low.

Stay on top of your Back Office
Check your Back Office several times a day to make sure you‚Äôre responding to messages as they come in ‚Äî and meeting the 24 hour response deadline. Just go to "Trade-in" > "Orders" > "Orders to reply to" and check if you‚Äôve got any messages.

Note that orders in this list are sorted from the oldest to the newest.

Make sure your ‚ÄúOrders to reply to‚Äù inbox stays clean
To do so, provide a valid response to all orders ‚Äî even if it means only saying ‚ÄúThank you.‚Äù You should also answer older orders, even if you‚Äôre already late.

Double check that you‚Äôre receiving email notifications
Make sure you‚Äôre receiving email notifications when a customer or agent contacts you about a Trade-in order. If you‚Äôre not, reach out to the Seller Support Center to get them set up .

Make a plan to tackle big issues

If you‚Äôre anticipating or encountering a big issue, you can consider the following options to solve it:
  • Adjust your process to handle orders received (including prioritisation of orders or a more organised workflow).
  • Check your ‚ÄúOrders‚Äù section more often.
  • Increase your resources temporarily.


‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚ÄãDon‚Äôt forget to plan your holidays
If you‚Äôre planning to go on vacation or be away from your business, we suggest you update all your listings to ¬£0 about 2 weeks before your departure. However, all customer messages must continue to be managed, so make sure you have resources to handle them during this period before you go.
¬Ý

How should I monitor my performance indicators?

You can check the progress of your Late Response Rate and other performance indicators in your Trade-in insights dashboard. You can access these from your Sales Back Office, then ‚ÄúInsights‚Äù > ‚ÄúTrade-in‚Äù.

If you have any questions, contact your Back Market point of contact or reach out via the Seller Support Center .
